Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand the key activities and their relationships The activities are OR MS QS AS IQC TST RT PK LB DP D Looking at the examples In Case 1 OR then MS then QS then AS then IQC then AS then TST then RT then TST then PK then LB then DP then D Case 2 skips LB Case 4 skips IQC and RT entirely

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the main sequence seems to start with OR then MS QS then AS

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: But after AS there is a loop with IQC In some cases IQC is done once sometimes multiple times Each time after IQC it goes back to AS

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So perhaps the loop is AS then IQC and if it fails you do AS again But in the logs after IQC it is AS again implying that IQC failed so they redo AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The problem statement says a loop star A B means execute A then either exit or execute B and then A again repeating until the loop is exited So in this case A is AS B is IQC

Name: Validation - IND
Text: In Case 1 AS then IQC then AS then TST so the loop would have been exited after the second AS

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Then after the loop exiting to TST there is another loop between TST and RT So the loop here is star TST RT

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Because you do TST then either exit to PK or do RT then TST again

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: LB is sometimes skipped In Case 2 LB is skipped So after PK there is a choice between LB and a silent transition

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So after PK there is X LB skip where skip is a silent transition Then after LB or skip you go to DP then D

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The main sequence is OR then MS then QS then the AS loop then the TST loop then PK then the LB choice then DP then D

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Case 3 has multiple IQC steps So the loop star AS IQC would allow AS then IQC then AS but in Case 3 it is AS then IQC then IQC then AS which is not possible with star AS IQC

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Therefore my initial assumption that the loop is star AS IQC is incorrect

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So B in the outer loop should be a loop on IQC Thus B equals star IQC skip Then the outer loop is star AS B where B equals star IQC skip

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This would mean do AS then either exit or do IQC loop and then AS again IQC loop equals star IQC skip means do IQC then either exit or do skip and then IQC again Since skip is silent the observable sequence for IQC loop is one or more IQC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So AS IQC loop would allow AS then IQC then IQC then AS which matches Case 3 Similarly AS then IQC then AS is possible and AS alone is possible as in Case 4

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For the testing loop star TST RT works because TST then RT then TST then RT then TST is allowed and matches Case 5

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore define IQC loop as star IQC skip define AS IQC loop as star AS IQC loop define TST RT loop as star TST RT and define LB choice as X LB skip

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main StrictPartialOrder contains OR MS QS AS IQC loop TST RT loop PK LB choice DP D with order OR to MS MS to QS QS to AS IQC loop AS IQC loop to TST RT loop TST RT loop to PK PK to LB choice LB choice to DP DP to D

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Case 2 Case 3 Case 4 and Case 5 are all matched by this structure
